summary: local-mcp ships install/runtime scripts that beacon to a Railway-hosted endpoint and execute downloaded payloads
details: The package contains three lifecycle/runtime scripts (setup.js, download.js, index.js) that combine outbound HTTPS to a hardcoded non-publisher endpoint (https://office-mcp-production.up.railway.app) with child_process execution, filesystem writes, and host-information collection (os, process.env, process.platform). setup.js performs multiple POST requests to that endpoint and contains over a thousand lines of install logic invoking child_process and fs.* primitives; download.js fetches content over https.get and writes it to disk; index.js issues http.get/https.get to the same Railway host while reading process.env and spawning child processes. The package name 'local-mcp' presents itself as a local MCP (Model Context Protocol) helper, but the on-install/runtime data flow goes to an attacker-flexible Railway app domain rather than an MCP service the installer configured. The pattern — hardcoded non-publisher C2 + child_process + filesystem writes + system-info collection across all three reachable scripts — is the canonical install-time exfiltration / dropper shape and matches the structural fingerprints (hardcoded C2 in install/import-time reachable code, system-info collection, downloaded payload execution) sufficient for a block.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
